items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what infrastructure or development work this contract covers.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant agreement
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to its consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, indicating ongoing work to improve traffic and safety at this major intersection. This project addresses coordination between vehicle traffic and railroad crossings on one of San José's key thoroughfares.

items.headline: City awards contract for local street pavement repairs
items.summary: The council approved bids and awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project. This infrastructure work will address street conditions across the city's neighborhood streets.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council reviewed Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, including edits and new scoring criteria to evaluate projects. This plan affects how Cupertino prioritizes biking, walking, and transit improvements across the city.

items.headline: City Council considers changes to vacancy filling process
items.summary: The council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled, potentially changing the procedure for replacing departing council members. This affects how residents are represented when seats open up.

items.headline: Major elevator upgrade project approved at Ridder Park
items.summary: A $1.1 million elevator modernization project has been permitted at 1290 Ridder Park Dr, suggesting significant investment in building infrastructure in that area. This scale of commercial upgrade typically indicates maintenance of an important office or commercial hub.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition lawsuit against city over Mary Avenue continues
items.summary: The city is in ongoing litigation with the Garden Gate Coalition regarding Mary Avenue safety concerns. This case suggests a significant dispute between residents and the city over street safety or development issues on that street.

items.headline: Commercial/industrial development coming to West San Carlos
items.summary: A new $696,570 commercial or industrial project has been approved at 1300 W San Carlos St. This represents new business activity rather than routine renovation or housing conversion.
